title: Trischidium limae

The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments, including wounds and infections. Trischidium limae contains bioactive constituents including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Trischidium limae include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Trischidium limae belongs to the family Polygalaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as milkworts or pygmy teasels. This family is part of the order Polygalales, which includes other similar plant families. Trischidium limae is a species within the genus Trischidium, a group of flowering plants native to the tropical regions of Central and South America.
